expectedDifferences.reason: The proxy stages a deterministic synthetic definition ID while Shopify returned the live-store definition ID.
The proxy returns the locally staged synthetic definition ID while Shopify returned the live-store definition ID.
The proxy returns the locally staged synthetic definition ID while Shopify returned the live-store definition ID.
The proxy reads back the locally staged synthetic definition ID while Shopify returned the live-store definition ID.

notes: Captures Shopify's idempotent standardMetafieldDefinitionEnable behavior for the PRODUCT descriptors/subtitle template on the 2026-04 target whose product-owner create/pin cap is 20 for ordinary definition pinning. The live setup temporarily unpins existing product definitions, creates 20 disposable pinned definitions, enables template 1, re-enables it, and re-enables it again with `pin: true`. Shopify returns the existing definition id, suppresses the pin-cap user error, assigns `pinnedPosition: 21` on the standard re-enable path, and still resolves the original id on downstream `metafieldDefinition(id:)` readback. Disposable pinned definitions are deleted and baseline pins are restored during cleanup.
